description: Processes a single child based on the branch index

parameterGroups.label: Branch Selection
parameterGroups.rationale: Single parameter controlling which child processes audio.

gaps.question: Compiled branch uses jmin(NumElements-1, idx) to clamp out-of-range indices to the last child. Does the interpreted BranchNode also clamp, or does it ignore out-of-range index values? What happens with floating-point Index values -- are they truncated or rounded?
gaps.context: Users need to know the safe range for the Index parameter and what happens at boundaries.

gaps.question: Confirm that all children are prepared and reset (even inactive ones), but only the currently selected child receives process() and handleHiseEvent() calls. Does switching Index mid-buffer cause a click?
gaps.context: The 'all prepared, one processes' model is important. Switching behavior affects whether users need soft_bypass for click-free transitions.

gaps.question: The compiled version has a static_assert limiting to 16 children (CASE_16 macro). Does the interpreted BranchNode have a similar limit, or is it unlimited? The parameter max is 10 by default -- does updateIndexLimit() adjust this when children are added?
gaps.context: Users need to know the maximum number of branches supported.

gaps.question: The infrastructure doc says BranchNode checks isBypassed() at process entry and returns immediately. Confirm: when bypassed, audio passes through unmodified (none of the children process)?

notes: Branch has HasFixedParameters=true in cppProperties, meaning parameters cannot be added/removed at runtime. The Index parameter's max value of 10 is a default -- the interpreted BranchNode adjusts this via updateIndexLimit() when children are added/removed. Compiled version limited to 16 children via switch/case macro.
